members.name: GetActionSets
members.summary: Returns a list of action sets registered with Godot (loaded from the action map at runtime).

members.name: GetAvailableDisplayRefreshRates
members.summary: Returns a list of display refresh rates supported by the current HMD. Only returned if this feature is supported by the OpenXR runtime and after the interface has been initialized.

members.name: GetHandJointAngularVelocity
members.signature: public Vector3 GetHandJointAngularVelocity(int hand, int joint)
members.summary: If handtracking is enabled, returns the angular velocity of a joint ([param joint]) of a hand ([param hand]) as provided by OpenXR. This is relative to [XROrigin3D]!

members.name: GetHandJointFlags
members.signature: public int GetHandJointFlags(int hand, int joint)
members.summary: If handtracking is enabled, returns flags that inform us of the validity of the tracking data.

members.name: GetHandJointLinearVelocity
members.signature: public Vector3 GetHandJointLinearVelocity(int hand, int joint)
members.summary: If handtracking is enabled, returns the linear velocity of a joint ([param joint]) of a hand ([param hand]) as provided by OpenXR. This is relative to [XROrigin3D] without worldscale applied!

members.name: GetHandJointPosition
members.signature: public Vector3 GetHandJointPosition(int hand, int joint)
members.summary: If handtracking is enabled, returns the position of a joint ([param joint]) of a hand ([param hand]) as provided by OpenXR. This is relative to [XROrigin3D] without worldscale applied!

members.name: GetHandJointRadius
members.signature: public float GetHandJointRadius(int hand, int joint)
members.summary: If handtracking is enabled, returns the radius of a joint ([param joint]) of a hand ([param hand]) as provided by OpenXR. This is without worldscale applied!

members.name: GetHandJointRotation
members.signature: public Quaternion GetHandJointRotation(int hand, int joint)
members.summary: If handtracking is enabled, returns the rotation of a joint ([param joint]) of a hand ([param hand]) as provided by OpenXR.

members.name: GetHandTrackingSource
members.summary: If handtracking is enabled and hand tracking source is supported, gets the source of the hand tracking data for [param hand].

members.name: GetMotionRange
members.summary: If handtracking is enabled and motion range is supported, gets the currently configured motion range for [param hand].

members.name: GetSessionState
members.summary: Returns the current state of our OpenXR session.

members.name: IsActionSetActive
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the given action set is active.

members.name: IsEyeGazeInteractionSupported
members.summary: Returns the capabilities of the eye gaze interaction extension. [b]Note:[/b] This only returns a valid value after OpenXR has been initialized.

members.name: IsFoveationSupported
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if OpenXR's foveation extension is supported. The interface must be initialized before this returns a valid value. [b]Note:[/b] When using the Vulkan rendering driver, [member Viewport.vrs_mode] must be set to [constant Viewport.VRS_XR] to support foveation.

members.name: IsHandInteractionSupported
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if OpenXR's hand interaction profile is supported and enabled. [b]Note:[/b] This only returns a valid value after OpenXR has been initialized.

members.name: IsHandTrackingSupported
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if OpenXR's hand tracking is supported and enabled. [b]Note:[/b] This only returns a valid value after OpenXR has been initialized.

members.name: IsUserPresenceSupported
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if OpenXR's user presence extension is supported and enabled. [b]Note:[/b] This only returns a valid value after OpenXR has been initialized.

members.name: IsUserPresent
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if system has detected the presence of a user in the XR experience.

members.name: SetActionSetActive
members.signature: public void SetActionSetActive(String name, bool active)
members.summary: Sets the given action set as active or inactive.

members.name: SetCpuLevel
members.summary: Sets the CPU performance level of the OpenXR device.

members.name: SetGpuLevel
members.summary: Sets the GPU performance level of the OpenXR device.

members.name: SetMotionRange
members.signature: public void SetMotionRange(int hand, int motionRange)
members.summary: If handtracking is enabled and motion range is supported, sets the currently configured motion range for [param hand] to [param motion_range].

members.name: DisplayRefreshRate
members.summary: The display refresh rate for the current HMD. Only functional if this feature is supported by the OpenXR runtime and after the interface has been initialized.

members.name: FoveationDynamic
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables dynamic foveation adjustment. The interface must be initialized before this is accessible. If enabled, foveation will automatically be adjusted between low and [member foveation_level].

members.name: FoveationLevel
members.summary: The foveation level, from [code]0[/code] (off) to [code]3[/code] (high). The interface must be initialized before this is accessible.

members.name: FoveationWithSubsampledImages
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], enables subsampled images with foveation, which can provide a performance boost on Vulkan.

members.name: RenderTargetSizeMultiplier
members.summary: The render size multiplier for the current HMD. Must be set before the interface has been initialized.

members.name: VrsMinRadius
members.summary: The minimum radius around the focal point where full quality is guaranteed if VRS is used as a percentage of screen size. [b]Note:[/b] Mobile and Forward+ renderers only. Requires [member Viewport.vrs_mode] to be set to [constant Viewport.VRS_XR].

members.name: VrsStrength
members.summary: The strength used to calculate the VRS density map. The greater this value, the more noticeable VRS is. This improves performance at the cost of quality. [b]Note:[/b] Mobile and Forward+ renderers only. Requires [member Viewport.vrs_mode] to be set to [constant Viewport.VRS_XR].

signals.name: CpuLevelChanged
signals.summary: Informs the device CPU performance level has changed in the specified subdomain.

signals.name: GpuLevelChanged
signals.summary: Informs the device GPU performance level has changed in the specified subdomain.

signals.name: InstanceExiting
signals.summary: Informs our OpenXR instance is exiting.

signals.name: PoseRecentered
signals.summary: Informs the user queued a recenter of the player position.

signals.name: RefreshRateChanged
signals.summary: Informs the user the HMD refresh rate has changed. [b]Note:[/b] Only emitted if XR runtime supports the refresh rate extension.

signals.name: SessionBegun
signals.summary: Informs our OpenXR session has been started.

signals.name: SessionFocussed
signals.summary: Informs our OpenXR session now has focus, for example output is sent to the HMD and we're receiving XR input.

signals.name: SessionLossPending
signals.summary: Informs our OpenXR session is in the process of being lost.

signals.name: SessionStopping
signals.summary: Informs our OpenXR session is stopping.

signals.name: SessionSynchronized
signals.summary: Informs our OpenXR session has been synchronized.

signals.name: SessionVisible
signals.summary: Informs our OpenXR session is now visible, for example output is sent to the HMD but we don't receive XR input.

signals.name: UserPresenceChanged
signals.summary: Signal emitted when the user presence value changes. [b]Note:[/b] This signal will not be emitted during application startup and application shutdown. Developers should assume user presence is gained on startup and lost on shutdown.

values.name: HAND_LEFT
values.summary: Left hand.

values.name: HAND_MAX
values.summary: Maximum value for the hand enum.

values.name: HAND_RIGHT
values.summary: Right hand.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_ANGULAR_VELOCITY_VALID
values.summary: If set, our angular velocity data is valid, otherwise, the angular velocity data is unreliable and should not be used.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_LINEAR_VELOCITY_VALID
values.summary: If set, our linear velocity data is valid, otherwise, the linear velocity data is unreliable and should not be used.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_NONE
values.summary: No flags are set.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_ORIENTATION_TRACKED
values.summary: If set, the orientation data comes from tracking data, otherwise, the orientation data contains predicted data.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_ORIENTATION_VALID
values.summary: If set, the orientation data is valid, otherwise, the orientation data is unreliable and should not be used.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_POSITION_TRACKED
values.summary: If set, the positional data comes from tracking data, otherwise, the positional data contains predicted data.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_POSITION_VALID
values.summary: If set, the positional data is valid, otherwise, the positional data is unreliable and should not be used.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_INDEX_DISTAL
values.summary: Index finger phalanx distal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_INDEX_INTERMEDIATE
values.summary: Index finger phalanx intermediate joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_INDEX_METACARPAL
values.summary: Index finger metacarpal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_INDEX_PROXIMAL
values.summary: Index finger phalanx proximal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_INDEX_TIP
values.summary: Index finger tip joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_LITTLE_DISTAL
values.summary: Pinky finger phalanx distal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_LITTLE_INTERMEDIATE
values.summary: Pinky finger phalanx intermediate joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_LITTLE_METACARPAL
values.summary: Pinky finger metacarpal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_LITTLE_PROXIMAL
values.summary: Pinky finger phalanx proximal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_LITTLE_TIP
values.summary: Pinky finger tip joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum HandJoints] enum.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_MIDDLE_DISTAL
values.summary: Middle finger phalanx distal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_MIDDLE_INTERMEDIATE
values.summary: Middle finger phalanx intermediate joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_MIDDLE_METACARPAL
values.summary: Middle finger metacarpal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_MIDDLE_PROXIMAL
values.summary: Middle finger phalanx proximal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_MIDDLE_TIP
values.summary: Middle finger tip joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_PALM
values.summary: Palm joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_RING_DISTAL
values.summary: Ring finger phalanx distal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_RING_INTERMEDIATE
values.summary: Ring finger phalanx intermediate joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_RING_METACARPAL
values.summary: Ring finger metacarpal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_RING_PROXIMAL
values.summary: Ring finger phalanx proximal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_RING_TIP
values.summary: Ring finger tip joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_THUMB_DISTAL
values.summary: Thumb distal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_THUMB_METACARPAL
values.summary: Thumb metacarpal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_THUMB_PROXIMAL
values.summary: Thumb proximal joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_THUMB_TIP
values.summary: Thumb tip joint.

values.name: HAND_JOINT_WRIST
values.summary: Wrist joint.

values.name: HAND_MOTION_RANGE_CONFORM_TO_CONTROLLER
values.summary: Conform to controller, if user closes their hands, the tracked data conforms to the shape of the controller.

values.name: HAND_MOTION_RANGE_MAX
values.summary: Maximum value for the motion range enum.

values.name: HAND_MOTION_RANGE_UNOBSTRUCTED
values.summary: Full hand range, if user closes their hands, we make a full fist.

values.name: HAND_TRACKED_SOURCE_CONTROLLER
values.summary: The source of hand tracking is a controller, bone positions are inferred from controller inputs.

values.name: HAND_TRACKED_SOURCE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum HandTrackedSource] enum.

values.name: HAND_TRACKED_SOURCE_UNKNOWN
values.summary: The source of hand tracking data is unknown (the extension is likely unsupported).

values.name: HAND_TRACKED_SOURCE_UNOBSTRUCTED
values.summary: The source of hand tracking is unobstructed, this means that an accurate method of hand tracking is used, e.g. optical hand tracking, data gloves, etc.

values.name: PERF_SETTINGS_LEVEL_BOOST
values.summary: The application has entered a section with very high complexity, during which the XR Runtime is allowed to step up beyond the thermally sustainable range.

values.name: PERF_SETTINGS_LEVEL_POWER_SAVINGS
values.summary: The application has entered a non-XR section (head-locked / static screen), during which power savings are to be prioritized.

values.name: PERF_SETTINGS_LEVEL_SUSTAINED_HIGH
values.summary: The application has entered a high or dynamic complexity section, during which the XR Runtime strives for consistent XR compositing and frame rendering within a thermally sustainable range.

values.name: PERF_SETTINGS_LEVEL_SUSTAINED_LOW
values.summary: The application has entered a low and stable complexity section, during which reducing power is more important than occasional late rendering frames.

values.name: PERF_SETTINGS_NOTIF_LEVEL_IMPAIRED
values.summary: The sub-domain has reached a critical level where the application should start drastic mitigation actions.

values.name: PERF_SETTINGS_NOTIF_LEVEL_NORMAL
values.summary: The sub-domain has reached a level where no further actions other than currently applied are necessary.

values.name: PERF_SETTINGS_NOTIF_LEVEL_WARNING
values.summary: The sub-domain has reached an early warning level where the application should start proactive mitigation actions.

values.name: PERF_SETTINGS_SUB_DOMAIN_COMPOSITING
values.summary: The compositing performance within the runtime has reached a new level.

values.name: PERF_SETTINGS_SUB_DOMAIN_RENDERING
values.summary: The application rendering performance has reached a new level.

values.name: PERF_SETTINGS_SUB_DOMAIN_THERMAL
values.summary: The temperature of the device has reached a new level.

values.name: SESSION_STATE_EXITING
values.summary: The OpenXR instance is about to be destroyed and we're exiting. [signal instance_exiting] is emitted when we change to this state.

values.name: SESSION_STATE_FOCUSED
values.summary: The application has synched its frame loop with the runtime, we're rendering output to the user and we're receiving XR input. [signal session_focussed] is emitted when we change to this state. [b]Note:[/b] This is the state OpenXR will be in when the user can fully interact with your game.

values.name: SESSION_STATE_IDLE
values.summary: The initial state after the OpenXR session is created or after the session is destroyed.

values.name: SESSION_STATE_LOSS_PENDING
values.summary: The session is about to be lost. [signal session_loss_pending] is emitted when we change to this state.

values.name: SESSION_STATE_READY
values.summary: OpenXR is ready to begin our session. [signal session_begun] is emitted when we change to this state.

values.name: SESSION_STATE_STOPPING
values.summary: Our session is being stopped. [signal session_stopping] is emitted when we change to this state.

values.name: SESSION_STATE_SYNCHRONIZED
values.summary: The application has synched its frame loop with the runtime but we're not rendering anything. [signal session_synchronized] is emitted when we change to this state.

values.name: SESSION_STATE_UNKNOWN
values.summary: The state of the session is unknown, we haven't tried setting up OpenXR yet.

values.name: SESSION_STATE_VISIBLE
values.summary: The application has synched its frame loop with the runtime and we're rendering output to the user, however we receive no user input. [signal session_visible] is emitted when we change to this state. [b]Note:[/b] This is the current state just before we get the focused state, whenever the user opens a system menu, switches to another application, or takes off their headset.

constants.name: SESSION_STATE_UNKNOWN
constants.summary: The state of the session is unknown, we haven't tried setting up OpenXR yet.

constants.name: SESSION_STATE_IDLE
constants.summary: The initial state after the OpenXR session is created or after the session is destroyed.

constants.name: SESSION_STATE_READY
constants.summary: OpenXR is ready to begin our session. [signal session_begun] is emitted when we change to this state.

constants.name: SESSION_STATE_SYNCHRONIZED
constants.summary: The application has synched its frame loop with the runtime but we're not rendering anything. [signal session_synchronized] is emitted when we change to this state.

constants.name: SESSION_STATE_VISIBLE
constants.summary: The application has synched its frame loop with the runtime and we're rendering output to the user, however we receive no user input. [signal session_visible] is emitted when we change to this state. [b]Note:[/b] This is the current state just before we get the focused state, whenever the user opens a system menu, switches to another application, or takes off their headset.

constants.name: SESSION_STATE_FOCUSED
constants.summary: The application has synched its frame loop with the runtime, we're rendering output to the user and we're receiving XR input. [signal session_focussed] is emitted when we change to this state. [b]Note:[/b] This is the state OpenXR will be in when the user can fully interact with your game.

constants.name: SESSION_STATE_STOPPING
constants.summary: Our session is being stopped. [signal session_stopping] is emitted when we change to this state.

constants.name: SESSION_STATE_LOSS_PENDING
constants.summary: The session is about to be lost. [signal session_loss_pending] is emitted when we change to this state.

constants.name: SESSION_STATE_EXITING
constants.summary: The OpenXR instance is about to be destroyed and we're exiting. [signal instance_exiting] is emitted when we change to this state.

constants.name: PERF_SETTINGS_LEVEL_POWER_SAVINGS
constants.summary: The application has entered a non-XR section (head-locked / static screen), during which power savings are to be prioritized.

constants.name: PERF_SETTINGS_LEVEL_SUSTAINED_LOW
constants.summary: The application has entered a low and stable complexity section, during which reducing power is more important than occasional late rendering frames.

constants.name: PERF_SETTINGS_LEVEL_SUSTAINED_HIGH
constants.summary: The application has entered a high or dynamic complexity section, during which the XR Runtime strives for consistent XR compositing and frame rendering within a thermally sustainable range.

constants.name: PERF_SETTINGS_LEVEL_BOOST
constants.summary: The application has entered a section with very high complexity, during which the XR Runtime is allowed to step up beyond the thermally sustainable range.

constants.name: PERF_SETTINGS_SUB_DOMAIN_COMPOSITING

constants.name: PERF_SETTINGS_NOTIF_LEVEL_WARNING

constants.name: PERF_SETTINGS_NOTIF_LEVEL_IMPAIRED

constants.name: HAND_JOINT_NONE
constants.summary: No flags are set.

constants.name: HAND_JOINT_ORIENTATION_VALID
constants.summary: If set, the orientation data is valid, otherwise, the orientation data is unreliable and should not be used.

constants.name: HAND_JOINT_ORIENTATION_TRACKED
constants.summary: If set, the orientation data comes from tracking data, otherwise, the orientation data contains predicted data.

constants.name: HAND_JOINT_POSITION_VALID
constants.summary: If set, the positional data is valid, otherwise, the positional data is unreliable and should not be used.

constants.name: HAND_JOINT_POSITION_TRACKED
constants.summary: If set, the positional data comes from tracking data, otherwise, the positional data contains predicted data.

constants.name: HAND_JOINT_LINEAR_VELOCITY_VALID
constants.summary: If set, our linear velocity data is valid, otherwise, the linear velocity data is unreliable and should not be used.

constants.name: HAND_JOINT_ANGULAR_VELOCITY_VALID
constants.summary: If set, our angular velocity data is valid, otherwise, the angular velocity data is unreliable and should not be used.
